title: cli: monero.key written with std::fs::write — uses default umask, spend key world-readable
description:
## Bug

In cli/src/commands.rs try_load_monero_keys(), when auto-generating a fresh Monero wallet on first use, the key file is written with:

    std::fs::write(&path, json)

std::fs::write uses platform default permissions (typically 0o644 on Linux — world-readable). The MoneroKeys struct contains spend_key_bytes and view_key_bytes in plain JSON. A spend key with group/world read permissions can be exfiltrated by any local user.

Contrast this with the identity.key save path (cli/src/commands.rs), which uses OpenOptions with .mode(0o600), and contacts.json which also uses .mode(0o600).

## Impact

On a shared system, the Monero spend key is world-readable. Anyone with local user access can read ~/.local/share/nie/monero.key and steal the funds.

## Fix

Use OpenOptions with .mode(0o600) when creating monero.key, mirroring the pattern used for identity.key.
